communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).

Please include all available information, including (if available):

• Date the request was filed
• Requester name
• Organizational affiliation of the requester
• Summary or description of request
• Request response date
• Status of the request
• Fee charged, if any
• Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable
• Whether any materials were released as a result of the request

Please provide the data in spreadsheet or machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or Excel).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Silvia Canelón

files.title: PA Right to Know Request  Form

communications.subject: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
THE CITY OF PHILADELPHIA      Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)    Your receipt number is: 35609  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com, 

   

 Thank you for your correspondence.  This is an automatic email confirming that your correspondence has been entered into our case management system for processing and response. If you have an additional request you wish to file, or if you wish to make a change to a pending request, please email it directly to righttoknowlaw@.phila.gov.     Did you know that the City already releases a lot of information and open data online?  You might be able to find the answer to your inquiry right now!   

   

 

Here are some common types of requests we receive and where to find this information online right now:  

 

 Search for a property's L&I related permits, licenses, violations, and appeals with the L&I property history tool.  

 

 Search for a property's Real Estate Tax Balance with the Revenue Department's tax real estate lookup tool.  

 

 Find all of the City of Philadelphia's open data sets, including City employee salary data, on Open Data Philly, the Philadelphia region's open data portal.  

 

 Search information about professional service contracts online at eContract Philly.  

 

 Search information about procurement contracts online with PHL Contracts.  

 

 Search City legislation and related hearings online with Legistar.  

 

 Obtain financial disclosure forms from the Department of Records.  

 

 

   If you seek a police incident report, you can obtain the proper form from the Department of Records.  Please note that police incident reports cost $25.      If you seek audio, visual, or bodycam records from the Philadelphia Police Department, you will need to make a request following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
CITY OF PHILADELPHIA  LAW DEPARTMENT One Parkway Building 1515 Arch Street Philadelphia, PA 19102  Renee Garcia  City Solicitor   BY EMAIL: 199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com Re: Mayor's Office - FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (Mayor's Office) Request #: 35609  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.  Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right-to-Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a Right-to-Know Request pursuant to the RTKL and will process it as an informal request.  You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal RTK Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.  However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov – please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.   The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here:   https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/   The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.  The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.  Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith    Chief Deputy City Solicitor    Right to Know Unit  City of Philadelphia Law Department    Tracking #: Com 35609 Request Summary: Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records: I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates). Please include all available information, including (if available): • Date the request was filed • Requester name • Organizational affiliation of the requester • Summary or description of request • Request response date • Status of the request • Fee charged, if any • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request Original Request:   

   

 

 

 

From:

199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com <199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com>

communications.subject: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request #36600
communications.communication:
THE CITY OF PHILADELPHIA      Re: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request #36600    Your receipt number is: 36953  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com, 

   

 Thank you for your correspondence.  This is an automatic email confirming that your correspondence has been entered into our case management system for processing and response. If you have an additional request you wish to file, or if you wish to make a change to a pending request, please email it directly to righttoknowlaw@.phila.gov.     Did you know that the City already releases a lot of information and open data online?  You might be able to find the answer to your inquiry right now!   

   

 

Here are some common types of requests we receive and where to find this information online right now:  

 

 Search for a property's L&I related permits, licenses, violations, and appeals with the L&I property history tool.  

 

 Search for a property's Real Estate Tax Balance with the Revenue Department's tax real estate lookup tool.  

 

 Find all of the City of Philadelphia's open data sets, including City employee salary data, on Open Data Philly, the Philadelphia region's open data portal.  

 

 Search information about professional service contracts online at eContract Philly.  

 

 Search information about procurement contracts online with PHL Contracts.  

 

 Search City legislation and related hearings online with Legistar.  

 

 Obtain financial disclosure forms from the Department of Records.  

 

 

   If you seek a police incident report, you can obtain the proper form from the Department of Records.  Please note that police incident reports cost $25.      If you seek audio, visual, or bodycam records from the Philadelphia Police Department, you will need to make a request following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Mayor's Office - complete copy of public record request log for the Mayor's Office from 1/1/24 to date
communications.communication:
Re: Mayor's Office - complete copy of public record request log for the Mayor's Office from 1/1/24 to date Request #: 36600  Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your inquiry* received on Feb 20, 2026. This correspondence constitutes the final response to your request by the City Law Department on behalf of its client, the City of Philadelphia. See Philadelphia Home Rule Charter, § 4-400.       Your request is granted. Responsive records are attached.       This correspondence will close your request with our office as permitted by law.   Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith     --  Margot B. Smith (she/her)    Chief Deputy City Solicitor – Right to Know  Compliance & Legislation Group  City of Philadelphia Law Department    1515 Arch Street, 17th floor     Philadelphia, PA 19102    Margot.Smith@phila.gov    RTKL Links: https://linktr.ee/phillylaw        ​*As explained in correspondence sent to you on Feb 27, 2026​, written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. § 67.101 et seq. In accordance with RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires use of the standard statewide Right to Know Form for the submission of written requests pursuant to RTKL (“RTK Requests”). See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file an RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Commw. 2012). This policy is available online at https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/ To the extent you intended to submit an RTK Request, your request is denied as you have not complied with the City’s Open Records Policy. As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and has processed it as an informal request. The City nonetheless referred to RTKL for guidance in determining whether records should be released in response to your request.                 

                           

                            On

                            Fri, Feb 27 at  9:44 AM

                            ,  City of Philadelphia Right to Know <righttoknow@phila.gov>  wrote:

                           

                           

                             CITY OF PHILADELPHIA     LAW DEPARTMENT   One Parkway Building   1515 Arch Street   Philadelphia, PA 19102     ​Renee Garcia   City Solicitor     BY EMAIL: 199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com   Re: Mayor's Office - complete copy of public record request log for the Mayor's Office from 1/1/24 to date   Request #: 36600     Dear 199616-62646222@requests. muckrock. com:     Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.     Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and will process it as an informal request.     You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal Right-to-Know Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.     However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov - please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.     The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here: https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/     The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.     The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.     Respectfully,     Margot B. Smith  Chief Deputy City Solicitor, Right to Know Unit, City of Philadelphia Law Department  (215) 683-5069 (fax)      Tracking #: Com 36600   Request Summary: I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).  Please include all available information, including (if available):  • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request   Original Request:  

 

 I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates). 

 Please include all available information, including (if available): 

 • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request 

   

 

 Mayor's Office RTK Office #204 1401 John F Kennedy Boulevard Philadelphia, PA 19107 

 February 20, 2026 

 This is a follow up to request number 36312: 

 To Whom It May Concern: 

 I wanted to follow up on the following Pennsylvania Right to Know Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 15, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response. You had assigned it reference number #36312. 

 Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed. 

 View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2223477/

 

 Filed via MuckRock.com E-mail (Preferred): 199616-62646222@requests.muckrock.com 

 PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS For mailed responses, please address (see note): MuckRock News DEPT MR 199616 263 Huntington Ave Boston, MA 02115 

 PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable. 

 ---

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: FOIA logs (01/01/2024 - present) (City Of Philadelphia)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).

Please include all available information, including (if available):

• Date the request was filed
• Requester name
• Organizational affiliation of the requester
• Summary or description of request
• Request response date
• Status of the request
• Fee charged, if any
• Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable
• Whether any materials were released as a result of the request

Please provide the data in spreadsheet or machine-readable format (e.g., CSV or Excel).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Silvia Canelón

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following Pennsylvania Right to Know Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 15, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.  You had assigned it reference number #Com 35597.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request #Com 35597
communications.communication:
THE CITY OF PHILADELPHIA      Re: RE: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request #Com 35597    Your receipt number is: 35816  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com, 

   

 Thank you for your correspondence.  This is an automatic email confirming that your correspondence has been entered into our case management system for processing and response. If you have an additional request you wish to file, or if you wish to make a change to a pending request, please email it directly to righttoknowlaw@.phila.gov.     Did you know that the City already releases a lot of information and open data online?  You might be able to find the answer to your inquiry right now!   

   

 

Here are some common types of requests we receive and where to find this information online right now:  

 

 Search for a property's L&I related permits, licenses, violations, and appeals with the L&I property history tool.  

 

 Search for a property's Real Estate Tax Balance with the Revenue Department's tax real estate lookup tool.  

 

 Find all of the City of Philadelphia's open data sets, including City employee salary data, on Open Data Philly, the Philadelphia region's open data portal.  

 

 Search information about professional service contracts online at eContract Philly.  

 

 Search information about procurement contracts online with PHL Contracts.  

 

 Search City legislation and related hearings online with Legistar.  

 

 Obtain financial disclosure forms from the Department of Records.  

 

 

   If you seek a police incident report, you can obtain the proper form from the Department of Records.  Please note that police incident reports cost $25.      If you seek audio, visual, or bodycam records from the Philadelphia Police Department, you will need to make a request following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. Sec. 67A03 at https://www.legis.state.pa.us/cfdocs/legis/LI/consCheck.cfm?txtType=HTM&ttl=42&div=0&chpt=67A. Such requests must be made in-person or via 

certified mail following the instructions in 42 Pa.C.S.A. §  67A(3) (aka

 Act 22). Such requests are not processed under the RTKL. See 42 Pa.C.S.A. § 67A(2)(a); 65 P.S. §§ 67.305, 67.3101.1. You may use this form to make a request under Act 22. Requests under Act 22  should be addressed to: Lieutenant Barry Jacobs, Philadelphia Police Department, Open Records/Right to Know Section, Philadelphia Public Services Building, 400 N. Broad St., 4W-72, Philadelphia, PA 19130. Such requests are not processed under the Right-to-Know Law.       Use the forms on this page to request copies of various public safety from the Department of Records, such as:   

 Traffic Accident Reports (aka "crash report" - also available through https://public-safety-reports.phila.gov/)  A records check or letter of good conduct from the Philadelphia Police Department  Fire Reports  Emergency Medical Services Reports       The City of Philadelphia does not process Right-to-Know requests for the organizations listed below.  If you want to submit a request to one of these entities, please click the links below to be directed to their Open Records Policies:   

 

Philadelphia Parking Authority  

 

 

School District of Philadelphia  

 

 

City of Philadelphia District Attorney’s Office  

 

 

Philadelphia Redevelopment Authority  

 

 

First Judicial District (Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas)  

 

 

   If one of these sources has solved your inquiry, please let us know!  Otherwise, thank you for your patience, and we will be in touch soon.   Sincerely,   The City of Philadelphia  Please Note: To help make sure that future emails from us don't end up in your spam or junk folder, please add this email address to your safe sender list.

communications.subject: Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date
communications.communication: CITY OF PHILADELPHIA  LAW DEPARTMENT One Parkway Building 1515 Arch Street Philadelphia, PA 19102  Renee Garcia  City Solicitor   BY EMAIL: 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date Request #: 36599  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.  Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right-to-Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a Right-to-Know Request pursuant to the RTKL and will process it as an informal request.  You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal RTK Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.  However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov – please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.   The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here:   https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/   The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. If you require a hard copy of the form and/or policy, please let me know and one will be provided.  The City reserves the right to assert any and all grounds of denial in its final response to your request.  Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith    Chief Deputy City Solicitor    Right to Know Unit  City of Philadelphia Law Department    Tracking #: Com 36599 Request Summary: Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:  I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).  Please include all available information, including (if available):  • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request Original Request:     Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:  I request a complete copy of any logs, spreadsheets, or databases maintained by this agency that track public records requests received from January 1, 2024 until this request is fulfilled (inclusive of those dates).  Please include all available information, including (if available):  • Date the request was filed  • Requester name  • Organizational affiliation of the requester  • Summary or description of request  • Request response date  • Status of the request  • Fee charged, if any  • Request System assigned identifier such as a Request Number, if applicable  • Whether any materials were released as a result of the request     February 20, 2026  This is a follow up to request number Com 35597:  To Whom It May Concern:  I wanted to follow up on the following Pennsylvania Right to Know Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 15, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response. You had assigned it reference number #Com 35597.  Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.  View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2223476/  Filed via MuckRock.com E-mail (Preferred): 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com  PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS For mailed responses, please address (see note): MuckRock News DEPT MR 199615 263 Huntington Ave Boston, MA 02115  PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share, and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed (i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.  ---

communications.subject: Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date
communications.communication:
Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date Request #: 36599  Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:  Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your inquiry* received on Feb 20, 2026. This correspondence constitutes the final response to your request by the City Law Department on behalf of its client, the City of Philadelphia. See Philadelphia Home Rule Charter, § 4-400.       Your request is granted. Responsive records are attached.       This correspondence will close your request with our office as permitted by law.   Respectfully,  Margot B. Smith     --  Margot B. Smith (she/her)    Chief Deputy City Solicitor – Right to Know  Compliance & Legislation Group  City of Philadelphia Law Department    1515 Arch Street, 17th floor     Philadelphia, PA 19102    Margot.Smith@phila.gov    RTKL Links: https://linktr.ee/phillylaw        *As explained in correspondence sent to you on Feb 27, 2026, written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. § 67.101 et seq. In accordance with RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires use of the standard statewide Right to Know Form for the submission of written requests pursuant to RTKL (“RTK Requests”). See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file an RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Commw. 2012). This policy is available online at https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/ To the extent you intended to submit an RTK Request, your request is denied as you have not complied with the City’s Open Records Policy. As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and has processed it as an informal request. The City nonetheless referred to RTKL for guidance in determining whether records should be released in response to your request.              

                           

                            On

                            Fri, Feb 27 at  9:45 AM

                            ,  City of Philadelphia Right to Know <righttoknow@phila.gov>  wrote:

                           

                           

                             CITY OF PHILADELPHIA     LAW DEPARTMENT   One Parkway Building   1515 Arch Street   Philadelphia, PA 19102     ​Renee Garcia   City Solicitor     BY EMAIL: 199615-39590937@requests.muckrock.com   Re: Law - copy of all public record request tracking data from 1/1/24 to date   Request #: 36599     Dear 199615-39590937@requests. muckrock. com:     Thank you for writing to the City of Philadelphia (City) with your request for information. This correspondence serves as an acknowledgement and receipt of your request as well as notice of the City’s Open Records Policy pursuant to the Right-to-Know Law.     Written requests for records to state and local agencies in Pennsylvania are generally governed by the Commonwealth’s Right to Know Law (RTKL), 65 P.S. §§ 67.101-67.3104. In accordance with the RTKL, the City has promulgated an Open Records Policy, which requires the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form be used for the submission of written requests pursuant to the RTKL. See 65 P.S. § 67.504(a); Dekok v. Lancaster County, AP 2010-1197, 2011 PA O.O.R.D. LEXIS 25 at *5 and n.1 (Pa. O.O.R. Jan. 17, 2011) (holding that in accordance with the RTKL, an agency may adopt a policy requiring the use of the standard statewide form established by the Office of Open Records in order to file a RTK Request); accord Pa. Gaming Control Bd. v. Office of Open Records, 48 A.3d 503 (Pa. Cmmw. 2012). As your request was not submitted on or with the standard statewide form, the City does not consider your request to be a RTK Request pursuant to RTKL and will process it as an informal request.     You do not have to resubmit your request for the City to respond unless you are specifically informed otherwise. The City reserves the right to require a formal Right-to-Know Request be submitted pursuant to the RTKL before releasing records. The City is continuing to process the request you have submitted as an informal request outside the RTKL and will be in touch with you further to respond to your request.     However, if you would like to file a RTK Request, please submit your request on or with the standard statewide Right-to-Know Form and email your request to righttoknowlaw@phila.gov - please do not reply to this email. Please include this notice with your RTK Request. Please note that if you resubmit your request as a written request pursuant to the RTKL, the City will deem your informal request to have been withdrawn.     The City’s Open Records Policy can be found here: https://www.phila.gov/open-records-policy/     The statewide form appears at the end of the City’s Open Records Policy. title: Drone as First Responder program contract and details

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Drone as First Responder program contract and details
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. A copy of all Requests for Proposal (or equivalent solicitations) and vendor bids and supplemental materials associated with this agency’s procurement of devices and services related to the establishment and execution of a Drone-as-First-Responder (DFR) program

2. A copy of this agency’s  contract (as well as all amendments, attachments, and exhibits) for devices and services used in its DFR program

3. Copies of all policies, directives, guidelines, and procedures governing this agency’s use of drones and the execution of operations under its DFR program, including documentation describing the device models and technical specifications of the devices used and any policies regarding the “curtilage” of a person’s property

4. A copy of all data sharing agreements and lists of other entities with which this agency shares or receives information. This includes the list of all agencies that receive notifications based on this agency’s list of license plates and vehicles of interest (often referred to as a “hot list”) and the list of all agencies that have access to all vehicle scans conducted by this agency’s devices

5. A copy of all manuals and operational references for this agency’s use of drones and the implementation and use of the DFR program. This includes all policies, procedures, and manuals related to the following elements of the execution of the drone and DFR program: 
a. appropriate deployment and use of the drone or drones; 
b. collection of camera footage, audio, and other data;
c. retention of footage, audio, and other data;
d. sharing of footage, audio, and other data, including via third-party networks and across jurisdictions; 
e. appropriate positioning of drone cameras and other payloads during flights;
f. the appropriate protection of collected, stored, and shared audio, footage, and other data
g. the policies and mechanisms used for auditing drone and DFR use, including appropriate investigatory use and access
h. the policies and mechanisms of consequences of unauthorized and inappropriate access to the drone or DFR systems

6. All materials sufficient to show the complete list of features and integration capabilities associated with the drone or DFR programs, including all materials related to device or DFR program integration with automated license plate readers (ALPRs), gunshot detection, video analytics, AI-enabled analysis, and other datasets, surveillance tools, and analytical systems.

7. All communications between a representative of this agency and a representative of the private entity from which this agency has procured DFR program devices and services for the period from January 1, 2025 through the current date.

8. A copy of the drone flight log for the period from January 1, 2025, through September 22, 2025 including the date and time of the flight, the investigatory purpose of the flight, the drone operator, and any other segregable information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Vivien Fleck
Please submit your request via our online portal through Sparkspolice.com. We cannot complete your request through fax.

communications.subject: Public Records Request 20253368
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Your public records request 20253368 is complete. You will receive a Sharebase link with the email portion of the request. You will receive policies, contractual information, and quotes from the PRR 20253368 which was originally submitted in September 2025. There are no data sharing agreements. Manuals and operational references are Skydio's documents and are on Skydio's website. Please also note the following information:

As required by NRS 239.0107(2)(d), please see the following notification(s).  Some records and/or information were withheld and/or redacted as confidential and/or privileged records for the following reasons.

Attorney-Client Privilege. See NRS 49.095; Seibel v. Eighth Jud. Dist. Ct. in & for Cnty. of Clark, 138 Nev. Adv. Op. 73, 520 P.3d 350 (2022); Wynn Resorts, Ltd. v. Eighth Jud. Dist. Ct. in & for Cnty. of Clark, 133 Nev. 369 (2017); United States v. Zolin, 491 U.S. 554 (1989)

Homeland Security & Confidential Tactical Operations. See NRS 239C.210 ("A document, record or other item of information...that is prepared and maintain for the purpose of preventing or responding to an act of terrorism is confidential...."); see also State of Nevada Executive Order 2020-01, Order Declaring Certain Public Safety Documents Confidential and Not Subject to Subpoena, Discovery, or Inspection by the General Public.

Individual Nontrivial Privacy Interest. See Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Rev.-J., 134 Nev. 700 (2018); Las Vegas Metro. Police Dep't v. Las Vegas Rev.-J., 136 Nev. 733 (2020); Cameranesi v. United States Dep't of Def., 856 F.3d 626 (9th Cir. 2017); Donrey of Nevada, Inc. v. Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630 (1990); see also FOIA Exemption 6 (5 U.S.C.A. § 552(b)(6)).

Proprietary Information. See NRS 332.061; NRS 333.333; NRS 338.1727; NRS Chapter 603.

If you have trouble accessing the documents or require anything further, please feel free to contact me back.

Thank you,

Kristin Lindquist
Police Records Supervisor
(775) 353-2241 ext. 5562
[Description: Description: Description: Description: Description: 60_60_90]

Bradshaw, 106 Nev. 630, 798 P.2d 144 (1990) ), information about victims of crimes (Nevada Constitution, Article 1, Section 23; NRS 178.5691); and information relating to individuals’ private interests such that disclosure to the public would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of privacy (Clark Cnty. Sch. Dist. v. Las Vegas Review-Journal, 134 Nev. 700, 429 P.3d 313 (2018)). 
 
 If your request contained any of the above information, it has been redacted or removed as required by law.

title: Upshur County, WV Opioid Settlememt Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Upshur County, WV Opioid Settlememt Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Samantha Smith
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12, 2026

Carol J. Smith, County Clerk
40 West Main Street, Room 101
Buckhannon, WV 26201 

To Whom It May Concern:

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Upshur County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you, Samantha Smith
samanthamsmithmedia@gmail.com

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Progress Park subdivision plat
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I'm Andy Kegley, resident of Wytheville in Wythe County and one of the leaders of Preserve Wythe, an ad-hoc group formed in response to the recent data center announcement.

I'd like to request a copy of the Wythe County Progress Park recorded subdivision plat, as referenced in section 13.2 Preservation Area (s) of the Wythe County Progress Park Protective Covenants, specifically with "preservation area (s) as designated," "owned and maintained by the County as historic preservation and/or interpretation areas."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Andy Kegley.

communications.subject: Re: **SPAM**  Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Progress Park subdivision plat
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

This email confirms that Wythe County has received your Virginia Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request. Under § 2.2-3704 of the Code of Virginia, we will respond within five working days.
If additional time is required due to the volume of records, the need to consult with other departments, or to determine the applicability of exemptions, we may invoke a seven-working-day extension as permitted by § 2.2-3704(B)(4).
If necessary, an estimate of fees for your request will be sent as soon as it is available. Our general FOIA policy and fee schedule is available at wytheco.org<http://wytheco.org/>.

Thank you,
Allyson Williams

communications.subject: RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Progress Park subdivision plat
communications.communication:
Thank you Allyson and others. While the wetlands covenants are nice, this still doesn't satisfy the question suggested in 13.2 of the Covenants for Progress Park, "Preservation area(s) as designated on the recorded subdivision plat of the Park shall be owned and maintained by the County as historic and/or interpretation areas." The Covenants were approved and recorded in 2001, these wetland plats weren't prepared until 2006. 
If such a designated area was never created or existed or this inclusion in the Covenants was simply a mistake, just say so. If I were receiving the Covenants, as did Mr. Noll and team last spring, I would be asking this same question. I did not see it addressed in the due diligence FOIA material provided to Mr. McWhirter in December, unless it was redacted. 
Best, 
Andy Kegley

communications.subject: Re: **SPAM**  RE: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Progress Park subdivision plat
communications.communication:
Good Morning Andy,

The provided wetlands areas are the only are the only recorded plats in relation to your request for preservation areas.  Although the Covenants reference conservation areas and a subdivision plat, we do not have record of one. We can provide plats of the individually sold lots within the park if you would prefer.
Allyson Williams

Public Information Officer/Grants Coordinator

Wythe County, Virginia

————————————————————————

290 South 6th Street

Wytheville, VA 24382

title: Mercer, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Mercer, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Alexandra Fohring 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March. 12 2026 

1501 West Main Street
Suite 121
Princeton, WV 24740

To whom it may concern 

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Mercer County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 

Sincerely, Alexandra Fohring 
lexifohring@gmail.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexandra Fohring

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wayne County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wayne County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Police data collection (2017-2022) (Issaquah Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. the total number of police use of force incidents reported from 2017-2022, separated by year.

2. the total number of police use of force incidents, separated by type of force and year, from 2017-2022 (for example, information that specifies the number of taser incidents in 2022, baton incidents in 2022, taser incidents in 2021, baton incidents in 2021, etc.)

3. the total number of civilian complaints against law enforcement that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year.

4. the total number of civilian complaints alleging law enforcement use of excessive force that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year.

5. the total number of civilian complaints alleging biased policing or racial profiling that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year.

6. the total number of civilian complaints alleging criminal conduct that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year.

7. the total number of officer-involved shootings from 2017-2022, both fatal and non-fatal, separated by year.

8. the total number of people who police used force against in 2022, broken down by race/ethnicity.

When available, I would appreciate being sent individualized data in this request in spreadsheet format. If individualized data are unavailable, please send information that includes aggregate statistics for the item instead.

Note that my requests for civilian complaints are specifically for civilian complaints, please do not include complaints filed by one officer/the department against another officer.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P018933-063024
communications.communication: SUCCESS! YOUR POLICE RECORDS REQUEST HAS BEEN SUBMITTED AND ASSIGNED THE REFERENCE #P018933-063024. We will contact you by 7/10/2024 regarding the status of your request.  If you not receive a response by this date, please email IPDRequests@issaquahwa.gov or call 425-837-3200. (mailto:IPDRequests@issaquahwa.gov) It is our goal to provide you with the records you are seeking as quickly as possible.  Please note that it may take us some time to locate, assemble, and review records prior to their release to you.  We will try to release records in installments as they become available.  The first installment of records is typically available within 2-4 weeks. We will keep you updated on the status of your request and any fees that may apply.  Please keep an eye out for emails or phone calls from us about your request and respond to any questions we may have at your earliest convenience. Your request will be processed in the order it was received, and alongside all the other requests for records.  If you have multiple records requests currently open with us, we will work on them one at a time in the order they were received unless you specify which request you would like processed first. Please note that copies of collision reports are $8.00.  The same report may be available for less through the Washington State Patrol's website or BuyCrash.com. You may contact us at any time regarding your request. Issaquah Police Records Unit Issaquah Police Records Portal  425-837-3200 You can monitor the progress of your request in " My Records Center".

Dear Samuel, Some of the records you've requested are now available for your Issaquah records request # P018933-063024 received on 6/30/2024: This installment of records includes an Excel spreadsheet that answers points #1, #2, #7 & #8. Since your total fees for the request are not yet more than $3.00, you will not be charged for the copies of the records provided today.  Fees may be owed for future installments. To view or download the records: 1) Log in to your records account.  2) Select View My Requests. 3) Select the View File(s) button in the bottom left of the tile with the #P018933-063024. 4) Scroll down to find the list of records. 5) Select either Download All or the blue text of a file name to download individual files. Note that some files cannot be viewed or opened on a mobile device. The next installment of records is expected to be available on or by 8/23/2024.  However, if you do not view or download the records provided today by 8/11/24, your request will be considered abandoned and it will be closed, with no further records provided per Section 3.8 of our records request policy.  Please reply to this email or call the number below if you have any questions or require any assistance. Best regards, Natalia Bebenek Police Records Specialist, Issaquah Police Dept. communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P018933-063024
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Dear Samuel, An update is available regarding your Issaquah records request #P018933-063024 received on 6/30/2024: To Whom It May Concern:" 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. the total number of police use of force incidents reported from 2017-2022, separated by year. 

2. the total number of police use of force incidents, separated by type of force and year, from 2017-2022 (for example, information that specifies the number of taser incidents in 2022, baton incidents in 2022, taser incidents in 2021, baton incidents in 2021, etc.) 

3. the total number of civilian complaints against law enforcement that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year. 

4. the total number of civilian complaints alleging law enforcement use of excessive force that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year. 

5. the total number of civilian complaints alleging biased policing or racial profiling that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year. 

6. the total number of civilian complaints alleging criminal conduct that were reported and the total number sustained from 2017-2022, separated by year. 

7. the total number of officer-involved shootings from 2017-2022, both fatal and non-fatal, separated by year. 

8. the total number of people who police used force against in 2022, broken down by race/ethnicity. 

When available, I would appreciate being sent individualized data in this request in spreadsheet format. If individualized data are unavailable, please send information that includes aggregate statistics for the item instead. 

Note that my requests for civilian complaints are specifically for civilian complaints, please do not include complaints filed by one officer/the department against another officer. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Samuel Sinyangwe 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fissaquah-police-department-32601%252Fpolice-data-collection-2017-2022-issaquah-police-department-166387%252F%253F&url_auth_token=AAGYJcaOGf_HXNMMFaUqqCKBmqQ%3A1sO6mh%3AcsVW7pqUcZmVmHyM0V1Y-2qzBBahR3B8qiydfEVwcC4 An installment of records was expected to be available by now. We now expect the records to be available on or by 10/4/2024. Thank you for your patience while we work to fulfill your public records request.  Please reply to this email or call the number below if you have questions or require any assistance. Best regards, Natalia Bebenek Police Records Specialist, Issaquah Police Dept. To view or download the records: 1) Log in to your records account. 2) Select View My Requests. 3) Select the View File(s) button in the bottom left of the tile with the #P018933-063024. 4) Scroll down to find the list of records. 5) Select either Download All or the blue text of a file name to download individual files. communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P018933-063024
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
Dear Samuel, The last of the records you've requested are now available for your Issaquah records request # P018933-063024 received on 6/30/2024: This final installment of records includes a table of civilian complaints broken up by year & categorized into 1 of 4 categories of type of complaint (use of force, criminal conduct, racial profiling & other). We would like to point out that after searching for responsive records & conferring with various employees, we were made aware that between 2017-2022 there was no formal log of civilian complaints so the information had to be pulled from various sources & compiled into the table we have provided to you. To view or download the records: 1) Log in to your records account. 2) Select View My Requests. 3) Select the View File(s) button in the bottom left of the tile with the #P018933-063024. 4) Scroll down to find the list of records. 5) Select either Download All or the blue text of a file name to download individual files. Note that some files cannot be viewed or opened on a mobile device. All locations where the records you've requested would reasonably be expected to exist have been searched and any records located that were responsive to your request have been provided to the fullest extent possible under the WA Public Records Act and City of Issaquah public records policy. If you believe that we have not provided all of the records you were seeking, please reply to this email or call the number below at your earliest convenience or submit a new request for the records: https://www.issaquahwa.gov/prr  This request is now closed.  The 1-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review of this request has now begun because the City does not intend to further address it. You are welcome to reply to this email or call the number below with any questions about this request within the next 20 days.  You may contact us any time with general questions about public records requests or to submit a new request for records. Best regards, Natalia Bebenek Police Records Specialist, Issaquah Police Dept. City of Issaquah, WA 425-837-3200

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Berkeley Housing Programs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records pertaining to contracts, programs, protocols of the City of Berkeley to provide housing and/or shelter services; including but not limited to financial assistance that has been supplied by the City of Berkeley.

All records that relate to the number of people in 2024 and 2025 who have been placed in permanent housing in part due to the part of the assistance of services.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Berkeley public records request #25-2916
communications.communication:
City of Berkeley ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-2916: This is in response to your November 25, 2025 Public Records Act request for: [To Whom It May Concern: Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: All records pertaining to contracts, programs, protocols of the City of Berkeley to provide housing and/or shelter services; including but not limited to financial assistance that has been supplied by the City of Berkeley. All records that relate to the number of people in 2024 and 2025 who have been placed in permanent housing in part due to the part of the assistance of services. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. Sincerely, Robbie Powelson Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2141059/] The City has identified documents which are responsive to your request. A partial production of those records can be found attached. The City is in the process of compiling and reviewing additional documents and anticipates being able to produce all responsive documents by February 20, 2026. If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page

Sincerely, Robbie Powelson Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2141059/] Your request is granted. The City has completed its search for responsive documents and as a courtesy has attached them to this letter. Certain records have been withheld based upon the confidentiality afforded medical or similar information, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, the official information privilege, and the confidentiality afforded to applicants for social services.  (Cal. Gov. Code § 7927.700; Cal. Evid. Code § 1040(a); Department of Motor Vehicles v. Superior Court (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 363, 373–74; see, e.g., Cal. Welf. & Inst. Code § 10850.)  With this response, staff have completed their work to process and respond to your Public Records Act request. If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov.   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Galveston County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Galveston County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned

communications.subject: 66075 JT RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits (Galveston County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Your request has moved to the next step in the process and has been turned over to the District Attorney’s Office who will subsequently refer to the Texas Attorney General's Office for an opinion to release or not. The law allows 10 business days for us to respond to your request and the District Attorney’s Office will contact you within that period. If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact Brent Haynes at Brent.Haynes@co.galveston.tx.us<mailto:Brent.Haynes@co.galveston.tx.us>. We appreciate your patience while we work to satisfy your request.

Thanks,


Crime Records & Analysis Division
Galveston County Sheriff’s Office
409-766-2320
Fax: 409-766-2295
gcsorecords@co.galveston.tx.us<mailto:gcsorecords@co.galveston.tx.us>

CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE:
The contents of this email message and any attachments are intended solely for the addressee(s) and may contain confidential and/or privileged information and may be legally protected from disclosure. If you are not the intended recipient of this message or their agent, or if this message has been addressed to you in error, please immediately alert the sender by reply email and then delete this message and any attachments. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, dissemination, copying, or storage of this message or its attachments is strictly prohibited.

15th Business Day:                             Wednesday, 03/18/26
Gov't Body Receiving Request:         Galveston County Sheriff
Record(s) requested:                           GCSO Flock Camera Data

Dear Sir or Ma'am:

Your public information request to the Galveston County Sheriff's Office was referred to our Office as legal counsel for the Sheriff. Please see the attached letter.

You will receive a copy of the Sheriff's brief when it is submitted to the Attorney General's Office. You will also receive a copy of the Attorney General's decision when it is issued.

Please note that further action on your request will be processed by outside counsel Olson and Olson. Please be alert to emails and other communications from Olson and Olson regarding your request.

NOTE:  Court records for Galveston County cases are available from the Galveston County Clerk (for misdemeanor cases; https://www.galvestoncountytx.gov/our-county/county-clerk/county-clerk-86) and the Galveston County District Clerk (for felony cases; https://www.galvestoncountytx.gov/our-county/district-clerk). Most court records are available online at: https://portal.galvestoncountytx.gov/portal. Case records may be searched by cause number or the defendant's name.

Brent Haynes
Asst. Criminal District Attorney
Criminal District Attorney's Office
Galveston County, TX
O-409-770-6280

title: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled (Department of Justice, United States Marshals)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Any memos, emails, and/or policies since January 20, 2025, related to the effort between ATF, DEA, FBI, US Marshals, and HSI to locate and interview at-risk unaccompanied alien children who entered the United States over the past four years.

- Any memos, reports, and/or emails drafted since January 20, 2025 quantifying the number and/or percentage of children identified as abused, trafficked, neglected, or exploited through Unaccompanied Alien Children Operations and/or other HSI wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children. 

- All trainings, memos, and/or policies used since January 20, 2025 about communicating with children and/or using trauma-informed interviewing skills provided to agents completing wellness checks on at-risk unaccompanied alien children.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lara S Nochomovitz

USMS FOIA Administrative Team
