communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: C305942-030926
communications.communication:
Dear Adam Steinbaugh: 
Thank you for contacting the City of Austin. Your request was received in this office on 3/9/2026 and given the reference number C305942-030926 for tracking purposes. Your request will be forwarded to the relevant City departments to locate the information you seek. 
Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

The settlement agreement in Andrew Fox v. City of Austin, No. 1:22-CV-00835-DAE, in the U.S. District Court for the Western District of Texas. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Adam Steinbaugh 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2236778/ 
Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, you will receive communication from the City of Austin within 10 business days informing you of one or more of the following: 
• The date the information will be available or a copy of the records sought 
• A cost estimate/invoice 
• A letter advising there is no responsive information for your request or that some/all the responsive information may or must be withheld by the City of Austin in accordance with the law 
• The need for clarification from you. 
Please note your request must ask for records that already exist. The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, perform legal research, or answer questions. 
You can monitor the status of your request using the "My Request Center" menu option at the Austin Public Records Center. 
You will receive an email once your request is complete.

- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers over the last 30 days

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Evan Cooper

title: Third Party Investigative Platform (Purchase and Contract records) (San Bernardino County Sheriff)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Third Party Investigative Platform (Purchase and Contract records) (San Bernardino County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the records listed below regarding the following four software programs: 

• Thompson Reuters Clear (https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/products/clear)
• LexisNexis Accurint (https://www.accurint.com/)
• TransUnion TLOxp, which may also be part of the TruLookup system (https://www.tlo.com/law-enforcement)
• SoundThinking CrimeTracer, which was formerly a product called COPLINK X (https://www.soundthinking.com/law-enforcement/crime-analysis-crimetracer/) from the company formerly known as ShotSpotter

For each of the above mentioned platforms, please provide the following records: 

• CONTRACTS AND AGREEMENTS —  All contracts (as well as associated amendments, attachments, and exhibits) and agreements related to the acquisition and use of these "software" programs.
• PURCHASE INFORMATION — All invoices and purchase information for costs and products associated with the above-referenced platforms.

Please provide responsive materials for the period from January 1, 2021 through the date this request is processed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Blum

San Bernardino County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #24-17987: Dear Requester: Please be advised that we are in the process of gathering records that may be responsive to your request. We will contact you again within twenty(20) days and provide you with an updated status. Sincerely, San Bernardino County Sheriff's Department ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at San Bernardino County.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #24-17987
communications.communication:
To whom it may concern,

Are you able to give me more details about the status of my request? I feel like the scope of what I am asking for does not justify the amount of time that fulfilling the request so far has taken, and I feel like I am being strung along by an automated system.

If there is something happening behind the scenes that is making my request take this long to fulfill, I understand, but I would appreciate being more in the loop with what is happening.

I look forward to hearing back from you about this.

Sincerely,
Aaron Blum

communications.subject: Your San Bernardino County public records request #24-17987 has been closed.
communications.communication:
San Bernardino County ************************************************************************ Record request #24-17987 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Thank you for making a request for records from the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department (Department). After reviewing your request, the Department has determined that the uploaded records are responsive to your request. If you believe this determination is in error, or if you have further information that you wish to provide to us regarding your request, kindly re-submit your request through our online portal: https://sanbernardinocounty.nextrequest.com/ You may also wish to review the Department’s Records Request Guidelines (CPRA) and/or the Department’s California Public Records Act FAQs information on our website at: https://wp.sbcounty.gov/sheriff/divisions/information-services/. Again, thank you for making a request for records from the San Bernardino County Sheriff’s Department. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at San Bernardino County.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: FLOCK - Greater Cincinnati (Fairfield Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The number of license plates scanned with Flock Safety automated license plate readers over the last 30 days
- The names of the hot lists/watch lists used with Flock Safety automated license plate readers 
- The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system
- All agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems
- All contracts, MOUs, or other agreements with Flock Safety
- All procurement documents related to Flock Safety

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Evan Cooper

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: 65-WFO-11874
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Copies of the following files: 65-WFO-11874 and 61-HQ-3499. Please include all sub files, bulky files, and enclosures behind file if they exist.

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Galante

    https://https://www.muckrock.comhttps://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?url_auth_token=AAAj2ProGzquHYFeb-SMkiAXsEc%3A1ihqBh%3ASbkvG6JbSMDwMGaBzDLmUThxXbM&next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Ffederal-bureau-of-investigation-10%252F65-wfo-11874-84599%252F%253Femail%253Dfoipaquestions%252540ic.fbi.gov

** 

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

** 

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1455124-000
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on "Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request" on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 104 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 853 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,786 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,500 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: Use of force data (2017-2025) Cities under 50k Population (Glastonbury Police Department)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Use of force data (2017-2025) Cities under 50k Population (Glastonbury Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Renard Spivey & Patricia Spivey Murder cases (Houston Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Renard Spivey & Patricia Spivey Murder cases (Houston Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The investigation of the shooting death of Patricia Spivey (victim) by her husband, Renard Spivey (suspect), at 5013 Briscoe St, Houston, TX 77033 on July 28, 2019. Incident occurred ~3:07 a.m.; Renard Spivey arrested July 29, 2019, and acquitted December 6, 2023, in the 185th District Court.​

Please provide copies of:

1. Audio recording of Renard Spivey's 911 call to Houston 911 at approximately 3:07 a.m. on July 28, 2019, reporting shots fired and accidental shooting.​

2. Home security camera footage (video and audio) from the residence, including:
(a)  3:03 a.m. clip of Renard Spivey in the kitchen moving to the bedroom.
(b)  Recordings capturing the couple's movements and relationship dynamics in the days/hours leading up to the incident (e.g., arguments over intimacy).

5. Post-shooting footage of Renard Spivey performing CPR on Patricia Spivey and taking a break to unlock the garage door (without informing the 911 operator).​​

6. First responder body-worn camera footage (HPD or HCSO officers) from arrival at the scene- Officers arriving at the scene, discovering Patricia's body in the closet with gunshot wounds, examining the crime scene, and interviewing Patricia's 83-year-old father (who was sleeping during the shooting) The location of the Renard Spivey and Patricia Spivey murder case scene is 5013 Briscoe Street, Houston, Texas 77033.

7.  Interrogation footage of Renard Spivey, after his arrest in late July 2019

We don't need written documents; we only need ( Audio/video) Files.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Danielle D.

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

The investigation of the shooting death of Patricia Spivey (victim) by her husband, Renard Spivey (suspect), at 5013 Briscoe St, Houston, TX 77033 on July 28, 2019. Incident occurred ~3:07 a.m.; Renard Spivey arrested July 29, 2019, and acquitted December 6, 2023, in the 185th District Court.​ 

Please provide copies of: 

1. Audio recording of Renard Spivey's 911 call to Houston 911 at approximately 3:07 a.m. on July 28, 2019, reporting shots fired and accidental shooting.​ 

2. Home security camera footage (video and audio) from the residence, including: 
(a) 3:03 a.m. clip of Renard Spivey in the kitchen moving to the bedroom. 
(b) Recordings capturing the couple's movements and relationship dynamics in the days/hours leading up to the incident (e.g., arguments over intimacy). 

5. Post-shooting footage of Renard Spivey performing CPR on Patricia Spivey and taking a break to unlock the garage door (without informing the 911 operator).​​ 

6. First responder body-worn camera footage (HPD or HCSO officers) from arrival at the scene- Officers arriving at the scene, discovering Patricia's body in the closet with gunshot wounds, examining the crime scene, and interviewing Patricia's 83-year-old father (who was sleeping during the shooting) The location of the Renard Spivey and Patricia Spivey murder case scene is 5013 Briscoe Street, Houston, Texas 77033. 

7. Interrogation footage of Renard Spivey, after his arrest in late July 2019 

We don't need written documents; we only need ( Audio/video) Files. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Danielle D. 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2143220/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant Houston Police Department department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Houston Public Information Request Center. 
Houston Police Department

title: Pocahontas, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pocahontas, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie Frederick
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026

Melissa "Missy" Bennett, County Clerk
900 C 10th Avenue
Marlinton, WV 24954

Dear, Melissa "Missy" Bennett
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Pocahontas County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.
Sincerely, Kylie Frederick
kyliejfrederick04@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Pocahontas, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie Frederick

Kyliejfrederick04@gmail.com

 

RE:   Freedom of Information Request Received March 12, 2026 VIA EMAIL

 

Please see the attached letter in response to your request.  In lieu of charging for FOIA documents as stated in the correspondence, I have also attached the funding reports at no charge.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Charlotte County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Charlotte County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA Request: Fender
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Metadata for email communications where Stephen Fender is either the sender, recipient, CC'ed, or BCC'ed, sent between the dates of 1/1/2022 and 1/14/2024 and containing one of the following keywords: "Democratic National Convention", "DNC" and/or "convention." Please include any variations that are not case sensitive.

The specific metadata data points I'm interested in are: to address(es), from address, cc address(es), bcc address(es), sent date/time, received date/time, subject line, and names of all attachments. This request does not seek the contents of the emails themselves, and only the specified email metadata. Please do not include duplicate emails. Please provide me with the records in an Excel format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Contracts with Palantir (West Virginia Intelligence Fusion Center)

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Contracts with Palantir (West Virginia Intelligence Fusion Center)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of contracts, including any post-signing amendment[s], addenda, new / later Statement[s] of Work, Change Order[s], and other documents that supplement or amend the original contract, between your organization and the company Palantir Technologies Inc. from 1 January 2020 to the current date.
- Copies of contracts, including any post-signing amendment[s], addenda, new / later Statement[s] of Work, Change Order[s], and other documents that supplement or amend the original contract that contain the word “Palantir” or “Foundry” or “Gotham” or “Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP)” from 1 January 2020 to the current date. 
- Any summary documents detailing payments that relate to the contracts above.

We give the above time frame because the company tends to engage in multi-year contracts.
I understand that some information may be exempt under current law; please provide all non-exempt portions of the documents. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Noam Perry

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Invoices, contracts, order forms, quotes, procurement documents between the Monterey County Sherriff's Office and Flock Group Inc, dba Flock Safety between January 1, 2023 and the date this request is processed.

2. The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system.

3. The names of all the agencies from which data is received through the Flock Safety automated license plate reader system.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Greene

title: Flock Safety and ALPR Data and Policies (South Gate City Clerk)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety and ALPR Data and Policies (South Gate City Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to the following records related to the  department’s Flock Safety and other automatic license plate readers (ALPRs), specifically:

1. FLOCK SAFETY INFORMATION
1.1. CONTRACTS and AGREEMENTS
1.1.a. All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, Statement of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, purchase orders, and other agreement documents with Flock Safety (‘Flock’, and/or ‘Flock Group, Inc.’) that are currently or were active any time between 1/1/2020 and the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates). This includes documentation related to the acquisition, leasing, and/or use of Flock Safety cameras or services, including associated data platforms and analytics tools.

1.2. GUIDING DOCUMENTATION and POLICIES
1.2.a. All policies, memorandums of understanding, and other guidance materials generated by the Department and/or any entity operating on its behalf regarding the acquisition and use of any data or services provided by Flock Safety (‘Flock’, and/or ‘Flock Group, Inc.’) and any representative thereof or any subdivision thereof. Responsive materials include, but are not limited to, any data-sharing agreements, retention policies, training materials, or other guidance materials.
1.2.b. All policies, records, emails, or data related to the sharing of passwords or otherwise offering access (e.g., performing searches informally by non-official request) with local, regional, or federal task forces or agents.
1.2.c. All non-email records detailing policies, SOPs, directives, training materials, and other guidance materials on when and how ALPR alerts may be utilized to initiate or justify traffic stops or detentions, requirements for independent corroboration or verification before enforcement action, documentation and/or reporting expectations for ALPR-related encounters, how hits are verified and how mismatches or false positives are handled, information on data retention and personnel access restrictions, and any policy memos explaining change history within any related documents.
1.2.d. All policies, SOPs, or other guidance materials generated by the agency detailing the required frequency of Organization Audits, Network Audits, and other audits of the Flock system and its use by personnel.

2. FLOCK SPECIFIC DATA
2.1. Organization Audit in Flock.
2.1.a. Flock-generated export report: This report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates). Per Flock’s documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the Department. Please include, at a minimum, the following fields: Name, Total Networks Searched, Time Frame, License Plate, Case #, Filters, Reason, and Search Date/Time.

2.2. Network Audit in Flock.
2.2.a. Flock-generated export report: This report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates). Per Flock’s documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the Department’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please include, at a minimum, the following fields: Name, Org Name, Total Networks Searched, Total Devices Searched, Time Frame, License Plate, Case #, Filters, Reason, and Search Date/Time.

2.3. List of Network Share Settings in Flock:
2.3.a. Data from Flock Platform: The names of agencies and organizations with which the Department shares ALPR data, also referred to as “Networks that I’m sharing.”
2.3.b. Data from Flock Platform: The names of agencies and organizations from which the Department receives ALPR data, also referred to as “Flock networks shared with me.”
2.3.c. Data from Flock Platform: The names of agencies and organizations with which the Department shares “hotlist” information, also referred to as “Hotlists that I’m sharing.”
2.3.d. Data from Flock Platform: The names of agencies and organizations from which the Department receives “hotlist” information, also referred to as “Hotlists shared with me.”

2.4. Hotlist Information in Flock.
2.4.a. Flock-generated export report: An exported log of hot list actions (typically “create,” “update,” and delete”) in the Department’s Flock Safety system spanning the last 12 months from the date this request is processed. This log should include fields detailing, at minimum, a timestamp, user performing the action, the event type (e.g., ‘create’, ‘update’, ‘delete’), entity type, and entity details (For Entity Details, please include at minimum the fields ‘hotlist name’, ‘reason’, ‘case’, and ‘expiry’). An example of this document may be viewed here: https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2025/08/23/CPRA_C001586_Hotlist.xlsx
2.4.b. Documentation regarding which external Hotlists the Flock system is configured to utilize.

2.5. Information on Users with Access to the Flock System.
2.5.a. Documentation indicating users with access to the agency’s Flock deployment and the access that is enabled/disabled for each user account. The report should include: User Id, Name, Role, Identity Provider, Last login, User Mgmt, Search, Device Mgmt, and Hotlist Tool.

3. GENERAL ALPR QUESTIONS
3.1. ALPR “Hot Lists”
3.1.a. A description of all “hot lists” or “custom hot lists” currently maintained for use in Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) systems.
3.1.b. Any policies, agreements, or data-sharing documents identifying external data sources used to populate those lists (e.g., DMV data on suspended/revoked licenses, NCIC, CLETS, Amber Alerts, stolen vehicle databases).
3.1.c. Any documentation offering guidance or specifying the criteria under which plates are added or removed from hot lists.
3.1.d. Any policies, SOPs, and/or software specification indicating how vehicles without plates or with plates that are unable to be read via automated software trigger alerts within system(s) utilized by the department.

3.2. ALPR Installation Locations.
3.2.a. Documentation detailing the location of all Flock camera and other non-mobile ALPR-capable recording device installations, both city-owned and non-city owned, that are located in Pawtucket with which the Department has access to (including via indirect access, such as the ability to query or otherwise view logged data or analytics). For each device, please include the following details: location of installation (provided with enough detail to make the location clear, either as streets at an intersection or geographic coordinates), street of which camera’s field of view covers, and direction in which camera’s field of view covers (using cardinal directions e.g., ‘Eastbound’), device make and model, and associated owner(s) and/or third-party service provider name(s), if applicable (e.g., Flock).
3.2.b. If available, documentation including but not limited to memos or similar which indicate the selection criteria that determined placement of installed ALPR devices.

GENERAL NOTES:
***Please provide all documents in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide files as they are natively output by Flock’s and other systems’ export tools (e.g., as .csv) where applicable. Otherwise, please utilize .pdf, .csv, .xls, .xlsx files as appropriate.

***If necessary, please forward this request or portions thereof to the appropriate custodian(s) (e.g., policy/training/legal custodians).

***If this request is denied in whole or in part, please justify all deletions and/or redactions by reference to the specific exemptions of the Access to Public Records Act. Additionally, if any documents are withheld, please inform me of the existence of such documents and the specific reasons for their being withheld.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jeffrey Lockett

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2219025/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please be advised that the District does not comprehensively track this information by student ethnicity/race and grade. Thus, this portion of your request is asking the District to create a new record to answer your question. 
The Freedom of Information Act requires a public body to provide existing public records, subject to certain exceptions. The request must reasonably describe the requested records. A reasonable description requires the record to be reasonably identified as a record not as a general request for data, information and statistics to be gleaned generally from documents which have not been created and which the [public body] does not generally create. (See Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195 (1980)). Second,  FOIA obligations apply to the provision of existing public records; a public body is not required to answer questions or generate new records in response to a FOIA request. Kenyon v. Garrels, 184 Ill. App. 3d 28, 32,    540 N.E.2d 11, 13 (1989) (quoting Krohn v. Department of Justice, 628 F.2d 195, 198 (D.C. Cir. 1980)). This request requires CPS to not merely pull data out of a database and provide it to you, but instead asks CPS to compile data from multiple sources into a single record, manipulate it to answer your questions, and this combination of data does not already exist in any CPS database. (See generally Hites v. Waubonsee Comm. College, Il App 2d, 150836 (2016)(... "requests went beyond a search for records—that is, the data in the databases—and instead improperly sought information about those records." quoting National Security Counselors v. Central Intelligence Agency, 898 F. Supp. 2d 233, 271 (D.D.C. 2012) and Chicago Tribune v. DFPR, IL App. (4th) 130437 at 8 (2014) ("[t]he Department was not obligated ... to answer plaintiff's "general inquiry question" concerning the number of initial claims since this would have required creating a new record.") 
You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

All records pertaining to Officer Anthony Simon, employed by the Kingston Police Department since approximately 2022:

All civilian complaints filed against Officer Simon, including complaint forms, investigation reports, witness statements, findings, and dispositions

All internal/departmental complaints or charges filed against Officer Simon

All disciplinary actions, sanctions, reprimands, suspensions, or other adverse employment actions

All use of force reports in which Officer Simon is identified as having used force, including supervisor reviews and findings

Sincerely,

EJ Fox

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Kingston PD: Anthony Simon
communications.communication:
I am appealing the Kingston Police Department's failure to process my FOIL request submitted December 18, 2025.

My request was in writing, reasonably detailed, and included my contact information—satisfying Public Officers Law § 89(3). 

The agency's demand that I resubmit through their preferred portal has no basis in the statute.

Additionally, the alternative contact the agency provided (nmalinoski@kingston-ny.gov) rejects incoming mail. See attached.
I request that my December 18 submission be processed as the valid FOIL request it is, with the statutory response period running from that date.

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Kingston PD: Anthony Simon
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This letter documents the Kingston Police Department’s ongoing failure to comply with the New York Freedom of Information Law.
Timeline:
	∙	December 16, 2025: I submitted a written FOIL request via email for records pertaining to Officer Anthony Simon.
	∙	December 18, 2025: Rather than acknowledging my request as required by statute, the Department responded by directing me to resubmit through an online portal—a requirement that does not exist under Public Officers Law § 89(3).
	∙	December 24, 2025: I appealed this improper response and noted that the alternative contact provided (nmalinoski@kingston-ny.gov) rejects incoming mail.
	∙	December 29, 2025: The Department has still not acknowledged my request, provided responsive records, issued a denial with appeal rights, or provided a written estimate for completion.

The statutory 5-business-day response period has now expired under any interpretation of the holiday schedule. The Department is in violation of Public Officers Law § 89(3).

I again request that my December 16, 2025 submission be processed immediately.

title: Wetzel County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Wetzel County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Addison Mikula
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12, 2026
Wetzel County Commission, PO Box 156
New Martinsville, WV 26155
To whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Wetzel County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through March 12, 2026
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, 
Addison Mikula
addisonmik@gmail.com

communications.subject: Police Records Request :: P000143-030426
communications.communication:
Dear Maxine Doogan: 
Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 3/4/2026 and given the reference number P000143-030426 for tracking purposes. 
 Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Please See attached PDF 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Maxine Doogan 

Attachments: 

https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/eroticprovider/172301/PRR_March_4_2026.pdf 
Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2233922/ 
 
The PRA requires that a local agency respond within 10 calendar days from receipt of the request, to notify the requester whether records will be disclosed (CA Gov't Code §7922.535).  The City responds as follows: Generally, the City does not have an obligation to provide documents that do not exist or documents that are exempted by statute. The requested information will be only provided to the extent that the information is not exempt from the California Public Records Act pursuant to any of the legal exemptions including, but not limited to, the attorney client communication and work product privilege, preliminary drafts, notes, or inter/intra agency memoranda not normally retained by the agency in the ordinary course of business, security procedures set forth by the local agency, investigatory materials, official information privilege, confidential peace officer records, records pertaining to pending litigation the City of Vacaville, the Vacaville Police Department or any employee is a party, or the deliberative process privilege. 
 
You have requested information which may not be maintained in a public record held by the Police Department. The Department has begun inspecting its records and will provide you with a response indicating whether or not any responsive records exist. 
 
Lastly, please be advised that the City of Vacaville charges $0.25 per page for copies of its records. Given the volume of records requested we will provide you with an estimate of the cost of the copies before we reproduce all of the documents. At that time you may determine if you would like a copy of all or any portion of the documents requested. 
 
Thank you for your kind attention to this matter. 
Sincerely, 
 
Vacaville Police Department

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Financial Reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NYDOT: Carrier Certification System Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request a bulk data extract from the NYSDOT Carrier Certification System (carcert.dot.ny.gov), maintained by the Office of Modal Safety & Security, including:

All fields maintained for each certified carrier: certification details, fleet vehicle inventory (count, type, make, model, year), insurance information on file, inspection history, and compliance status
For all currently certified and previously certified carriers (active, suspended, revoked, and expired)
I request this data in electronic format (CSV, pipe-delimited text, or database export). I understand that certain fields may contain personally identifiable information (driver records, Article 19-A records) -- I am not requesting those fields. I am requesting carrier-level organizational and fleet data only.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

communications.communication:
RE: FOIL Records Request of February 16, 2026, Reference # R010048-021626 
Dear Ian White, 
This correspondence will acknowledge receipt of your February 16, 2026 Freedom of Information (FOIL) request at the New York State Department of Transportation (NYSDOT) Records Access Office on this date. 
You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

I request a bulk data extract from the NYSDOT Carrier Certification System (carcert.dot.ny.gov), maintained by the Office of Modal Safety & Security, including: 

All fields maintained for each certified carrier: certification details, fleet vehicle inventory (count, type, make, model, year), insurance information on file, inspection history, and compliance status 
For all currently certified and previously certified carriers (active, suspended, revoked, and expired) 
I request this data in electronic format (CSV, pipe-delimited text, or database export). I understand that certain fields may contain personally identifiable information (driver records, Article 19-A records) -- I am not requesting those fields. I am requesting carrier-level organizational and fleet data only. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Ian White 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2219155/" 
We are researching your request and will notify you within the next 20 business days regarding the availability of the records you are seeking. 
Sincerely, 
New York State Department of Transportation

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: NYDOT: Truck/Freight Carrier Operator Profiles
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

NYSDOT's Truck & Motor Carrier Safety website states that truck and freight carrier operator profiles are "available by written request." I am requesting a bulk dataset of all truck/freight carrier operator profiles maintained by NYSDOT's Office of Modal Safety & Security, Truck & Motor Carrier Safety division, including:

Carrier / operator name
NYSDOT registration or permit number
Business address
USDOT number (if on file)
Number of vehicles
Vehicle types operated
Operating authority type (intrastate, for-hire, private, etc.)
Registration status (active, expired, suspended)
Registration effective and expiration dates
Any safety ratings or compliance status
I request this data in electronic format (CSV or pipe-delimited text file).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

title: Records retention schedule (Department of Housing and Urban Development)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records retention schedule (Department of Housing and Urban Development)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all materials, indices, directives, manuals, and other guiding documentation regarding records generated and retained, including but not limited to: 

• Record retention schedules
• Any other data structures used for the management of records: digital, paper, and otherwise

I request that you release records for this request on a rolling basis as they become available and in electronic form. If your agency does not maintain these public records, please let me know who does and include the proper custodian's name and address. If you choose to deny this request, please provide a written explanation for the denial including a reference to the specific statutory exemption(s) upon which you rely. Also, please provide all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. 

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Charles Swanson
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12 2026

Sherry Simmons, County Clerk
211 First Street, Suite 205
Parsons, WV 26287

To whom it may concern,

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Tucker County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 

- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026

- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to date March 12, 2026
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Charles Swanson
304-771-3317
charlesswanson101@gmail.com

communications.subject: Re: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Please find the attached documents requested on March 16, 2026
Thank you,
Barbara Evans
Tucker County Clerk

title: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (Office of the Mayor of San Francisco)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (Office of the Mayor of San Francisco)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) discussing procurement, purchase, installation, operating procedures, presentations, and policy relating to the use and deployment of Flock ALPRs
2) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between your office's personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple office members. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide a detailed explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld.
In the event that you determine SFPD does not handle or maintain certain records or portions of records, please indicate which office or department does.

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest about the operations of a government agency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Montgomery County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Montgomery County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Brooke County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Brooke County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Addison Mikula 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12 2026
Brooke County Clerk's Office
632 Main Street, Suite 201
Wellsburg, WV 26070 

Dear whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Brooke County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026.
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to date FOIA was sent
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Addison Mikula
addisonmik@gmail.com

communications.communication:

Kimberly M. Barbetta

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: James W. McCord Jr.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to James Walter McCord Jr. (died June 15, 2017). Mr. McCord served as an FBI special agent and was one of the Watergate burglars. 

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

REQUEST FOR FEE WAIVER

I request that all fees related to the processing of this request be waived. As one of the Watergate burglars, James McCord was a key player in a significant moment in American history. And because Mr. McCord was an FBI employee, the Bureau's records are likely to provide the public with significant insight into that moment. I am not making this request for commercial purposes— I am making it so that responsive records will be easily available to students, historians, journalists, and the general public. All responsive documents will be made available to the general public online for free. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges before fulfilling my request.

I would prefer that this request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or by CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz

    All records related to James Walter McCord Jr. (died June 15, 2017). Mr.
    McCord served as an FBI special agent and was one of the Watergate burglars.

    Nicholas Marritz
    
    Attachments:
    
    
    https://cdn.muckrock.com/outbound_composer_attachments/nmarritz/70580/WaPo20Obit20-20James20McCord20Jr..pdf
    Upload documents directly:
    https://https://www.muckrock.comhttps://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?url_auth_token=AAAj2ProGzquHYFeb-SMkiAXsEc%3A1hR7a9%3ANu_Bidr1RKxbVy89l7kQvL6v2eA&next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Ffederal-bureau-of-investigation-10%252Fjames-w-mccord-jr-73215%252F%253Femail%253Dfoipaquestions%252540ic.fbi.gov

Supporting documentation **

Please be advised that efoia@subscriptions.fbi.gov is a no-reply email
address. Questions regarding your FOIA request may be directed to
foipaquestions@fbi.gov. If you have received a FOIPA request number, please
include this in all correspondence concerning your request. Please note eFOIPA
requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

** 

Upon receipt of your FOIPA request number, you may check the status of your
FOIPA request on the FBI’s electronic FOIA Library (The Vault) on the FBI’s
public website, http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on the ‘Check Status of Your
FOI/PA Request tool’ link. Status updates are performed on a weekly basis. If
you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1437109-000
communications.communication:
Re: FOIPA Request No.: 1437109-000

Dear FBI:

In response to my above-numbered FOIA request for records relating to James Walter McCord, Jr., I received the following response:

"In an effort to provide you with responsive records as expeditiously as possible, we are releasing documents from previous requests regarding your subject. We consider your request fulfilled. Since we relied on previous results, additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does
not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."  

I hereby request an additional search for records. Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

Thank you very much.

Most sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz
